title: Application of pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC) for the determination of some important antibiotics
author: Bijyendra Kumar Dubey
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry University of Allahabad, Allahabad-211 002, India 

  Manuscript received 28 March 2003, revised 24 November 2003, accepted 2 December 2003  

 A quick and convenient method has been developed for determination of some antibiotic drugs in low concentration. Aliquots containing 1-5 mg of the antibiotics are allowed to react with 3 x 10 -2   N  pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC) in the presence of 5  N  H 2 SO 4  for 5-60 min at RT (25-30°). After the reaction is over, the unconsumed reagent is back determined iodometrically. The value of % error, SD and CV prove the method to be precise and reproducible.
